AFU6TQ6LMZ2JTGWXLCAOI256QZPA.product_description: This is the Android version of Freebloks 3D, an implementation of the famous board game Blokus by Sekkoïa. Try to place as many tiles on the board as possible, keeping in mind only two simple rules: your tiles must touch a corner of one of your previously placed tiles, but they must not share an edge. Can you play more tiles than your opponents? RULESEach player has 21 Tetris-like tiles: 12 tiles with 5 squares, 5 tiles with 4 squares, 2 tiles with 3 squares, 1 tile with 2 squares and 1 tile with 1 square. Players take turns in placing one tile onto the 20x20 board. The first tile for each player has to be placed in their corner of the board. Each following tile has to touch a corner of one of your previous tiles, but it must never share an edge. It may share edges with the opponents' tiles though. If a player has no more possible move, they have to pass. The game is over when no player can place a tile. For each player, the squares of all their remaining tiles are added up. The player with the lowest number of squares left wins. HOW TO PLAY- Swipe the list of available tiles with your finger. - Select and drag a tile onto the board. - Rotate or flip the tile using the 4 handles. - Place the tile in the desired position. The tile will appear green if the position is valid, and red otherwise. Possible corners are highlighted on the board for convenience. - Tap the tile in a valid position to place it. - You can rotate the board at any time to see the opponents' tiles. Quit the app at any time, your current game will be saved and restored on next start. FEATURES- Supports 2-player mode, either with 2 colors (Blokus Duo, Blokus Junior) or 4 colors (2 each).- Customizable board size (other than 20x20). - Play against the computer or humans on the same device. - Play online against your friends.- Hint and undo options.The game is network-compatible with Freebloks 3D for Windows and Linux: http://www.saschahlusiak.de/freebloks-3d/ The game is absolutely free and without advertisement. The complete source code is available on GitHub: https://github.com/shlusiak/Freebloks-Android If you have any idea, feature request or wish concerning the game, just drop us an email: mail@saschahlusiak.de

AFTJ7XQN2EIELHARUBBL6Y7L65DA.product_description: Pik Qwik - Tile Word Game - A Whirlwind Word Game w/ 4 Categories of Fun! Flip Over a Card and be First to Make a Word from The Letter Tile Pile in The Middle. Hit The Buzzer When You do and Score!
AFTJ7XQN2EIELHARUBBL6Y7L65DA.review_text:
The presentation/storage of this game is unique and pretty cool. The bottom (card holder) compartment is WAY more difficult to open than the other two (buzzer + tile holder) compartments, though. I'm not sure why. I actually had two copies of this game. The original one had a broken buzzer -- the power button was loose and maybe not even connected internally. So, I gave up trying to open the bottom compartment on that one since I was returning it anyway. The second (replacement) game had a working buzzer, but that bottom compartment still seemed to be impossibly sealed. Eventually we got it open, but the next time we wanted to play, it was once again difficult to open, even though we hadn't tried to close it tightly.
The directions are annoying -- the pages go from right to left. This seems especially weird for a word game. The buzzer makes a quick beep, which isn't very satisfying -- smacking the table or yelling out a word would be just as effective. But the buzzer is a fun way to top the game container, as long as it doesn't get bumped around in a toy box. The dual languages on the game cards is another interesting feature, but also a bit confusing at times during a fast paced game.
The container and game pieces and cards are all durable and well-made.
Gameplay is simple, but enjoyable.
Overall, this is a fun game which can basically be as long or short as you'd like it to be each time you play.
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity

AFTJ7XQN2EIELHARUBBL6Y7L65DA.product_description: Powkiddy RGB20S Handheld Retro Game Console with Built-in Games,3.5 Inch IPS Screen Game Player (128G 20000 Games Yellow)
AFTJ7XQN2EIELHARUBBL6Y7L65DA.review_text:
This game "console" is lightweight and doesn't feel high quality, but I expected that.
There are lots and lots of included games -- many familiar, some new-to-me. (Super Mario 14!)
The graphics are in color, pixel-y, and there is some odd lag -- especially in some of the side scrollers, which seem to update the background in a diagonal wave.
There are no instructions--paper or digital--for the individual games.
The charge port is on the top of the handheld device, not the bottom as shown on the included direction sheet. We cannot get the controller to work with the game. Maybe it only works if the unit is plugged into a television? It plugs into the charging port on the top of the game, so it would be awkward to use with the handheld screen anyway.
Overall, the games are fun, and this is a nice, lightweight time-waster/boredom-buster toy.
